title: Bardella's contradiction of Le Pen on pensions confounds French far right
subhead: Jordan Bardella, who would stand as the Rassemblement National's presidential candidate if Marine Le Pen's election ban is upheld, has broken with his boss's line on a crucial policy issue.

title: US, Iran hit each other again as hopes for quick peace deal fade

'The egalitarianism of hunter-gatherer societies has often been exaggerated'
subhead: The myth of the 'noble savage,' which suggests that humanity is naturally egalitarian, is challenged by a new study. Drawing on traditional societies, it explains that individual motivations are always behind human actions, reports Pauline Grosjean, in her column.

title: United Kingdom faces new night of anti-immigrant riots in Belfast, following a now-familiar patten
subhead: After a Sudanese man stabbed a Northern Irish man, dozens of masked men targeted homes of people of foreign origin. As with recent similar events, far-right activists are exploiting the incident and sharing it on social media.

title: Knicks stage historic comeback to beat Spurs, one win from NBA title
subhead: OG Anunoby's tip-in basket with 1.2 seconds left sealed the thrilling victory to give the Knicks a 3-1 stranglehold on the best-of-seven championship series, which shifts back to San Antonio for game five on Saturday.

title: 'In liberal democracies, the grace period for leaders rarely lasts more than six months'
subhead: Financial crises, wars, Covid: Over the past several years, an uninterrupted series of external shocks has eroded citizens' trust in representative democracy, observes Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon.

'While the G7 is indisputably obsolescent, nothing seems capable of replacing it'
subhead: Although rooted in a bygone era and battered by Donald Trump, the Group of Seven continues to meet purely out of habit, writes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris, one week before the summit chaired by French President Emmanuel Macron in Evian.

title: 'Disclosure Day': Steven Spielberg goes back to the future
subhead: After two more personal, retrospective feature films, the American filmmaker revisits his passion for extraterrestrials.

title: 'The cultural boycott of Israeli director Nadav Lapid is an intellectual failure'
subhead: Russian, Israeli and Iranian filmmakers should not be threatened with erasure to atone for crimes committed by governments they often fiercely oppose, argue a group of film figures, including Natalie Portman, Justine Triet and Jacques Audiard, in an open letter.
